title: Set up Sanity CMS in a Next.js project
summary: Complete Sanity CMS integration for Next.js — project creation, Studio embedding, schema authoring, GROQ queries, Live Preview, and webhook-driven revalidation.
declaration: I will set up Sanity CMS in this Next.js project. This includes creating a Sanity project, embedding Sanity Studio at /studio, defining content schemas, fetching content with GROQ, and wiring up on-demand revalidation via webhooks. Let me know if you already have a Sanity project ID — otherwise I will guide you through creating one.
instructions:
## Set Up Sanity CMS in a Next.js Project ### 1. Pre-flight checks - Verify `package.json` exists and the project is a Next.js 13+ app (App Router recommended — check for `app/` directory). - Check if Sanity packages are already installed: look for `next-sanity` or `@sanity/client` in dependencies. If found, skip to step 4. - Determine the package manager in use: check for `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `yarn.lock`, `bun.lockb`, or `package-lock.json`. Use the matching installer. - If the user does not have a Sanity project yet, tell them to run `npx sanity@latest init` separately — this creates the project on sanity.io and returns a `projectId`. You need the `projectId` and `dataset` name before continuing. ### 2. Install dependencies - Install the official Next.js Sanity integration: ``` npm install next-sanity @sanity/image-url ``` - Install Sanity UI and icon packages (needed for Studio): ``` npm install @sanity/ui @sanity/icons ``` (Substitute `npm` with the detected package manager.) ### 3. Configure environment variables - Create or update `.env.local` with: ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_PROJECT_ID=your_project_id NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_DATASET=production SANITY_API_READ_TOKEN=your_read_token SANITY_WEBHOOK_SECRET=your_webhook_secret ``` - `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefixed vars are exposed to the browser (safe — project ID and dataset are not secrets). - `SANITY_API_READ_TOKEN` is server-only; generate it in the Sanity dashboard under API → Tokens with "Viewer" permission. - `SANITY_WEBHOOK_SECRET` is used to validate incoming revalidation webhooks. Generate a random string: `openssl rand -hex 32`. - Add all four lines to `.env.example` with placeholder values for teammates. ### 4. Create the Sanity client - Create `src/sanity/client.ts` (or `lib/sanity/client.ts` matching project structure): ```typescript import { createClient } from 'next-sanity' export const client = createClient({ projectId: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_PROJECT_ID!, dataset: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_DATASET!, apiVersion: '2024-01-01', useCdn: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production', }) ``` - `apiVersion` pins the API version — use today's date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. - `useCdn: true` in production serves cached content instantly. Set to `false` for draft/preview mode. ### 5. Define content schemas - Create `src/sanity/schemaTypes/post.ts` as a starter schema: ```typescript import { defineField, defineType } from 'sanity' export const postType = defineType({ name: 'post', title: 'Post', type: 'document', fields: [ defineField({ name: 'title', type: 'string', validation: (rule) => rule.required(), }), defineField({ name: 'slug', type: 'slug', options: { source: 'title' }, validation: (rule) => rule.required(), }), defineField({ name: 'body', type: 'array', of: [{ type: 'block' }], }), defineField({ name: 'publishedAt', type: 'datetime', }), ], }) ``` - Create `src/sanity/schemaTypes/index.ts` to export all types: ```typescript import { postType } from './post' export const schemaTypes = [postType] ``` - Add more document types as needed. Schema files are TypeScript — type safety is built in. ### 6. Create the Sanity config file - Create `src/sanity/sanity.config.ts`: ```typescript import { defineConfig } from 'sanity' import { structureTool } from 'sanity/structure' import { visionTool } from '@sanity/vision' import { schemaTypes } from './schemaTypes' export default defineConfig({ name: 'default', title: 'My Project', projectId: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_PROJECT_ID!, dataset: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_DATASET!, plugins: [structureTool(), visionTool()], schema: { types: schemaTypes }, }) ``` ### 7. Embed Sanity Studio in Next.js - Create the catch-all Studio route at `app/studio/[[...tool]]/page.tsx`: ```tsx 'use client' import { NextStudio } from 'next-sanity/studio' import config from '@/sanity/sanity.config' export default function StudioPage() { return } ``` - This embeds the full Sanity Studio at `localhost:3000/studio`. No separate Studio server needed. - The `[[...tool]]` catch-all is required — Sanity Studio uses sub-routes internally. - Add a `metadata` export if you want a custom page title: ```tsx export const metadata = { title: 'Sanity Studio' } ``` ### 8. Fetch content with GROQ - Create `src/sanity/queries.ts` for reusable queries: ```typescript import { groq } from 'next-sanity' export const postsQuery = groq` *[_type == "post"] | order(publishedAt desc) { _id, title, slug, publishedAt } ` export const postBySlugQuery = groq` *[_type == "post" && slug.current == $slug][0] { _id, title, slug, body, publishedAt } ` ``` - Fetch in a Server Component: ```tsx import { client } from '@/sanity/client' import { postsQuery } from '@/sanity/queries' export default async function BlogPage() { const posts = await client.fetch(postsQuery) return (
    {posts.map((post: any) => (
  • {post.title}
    • ))}
) } ``` - GROQ (`*[_type == "post"]`) is Sanity's query language. It is powerful but different from SQL — start simple. ### 9. Configure on-demand revalidation via webhook - Create the webhook handler at `app/api/revalidate/route.ts`: ```typescript import { revalidatePath, revalidateTag } from 'next/cache' import { type NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server' import { parseBody } from 'next-sanity/webhook' export async function POST(req: NextRequest) { try { const { isValidSignature, body } = await parseBody<{ _type: string }>( req, process.env.SANITY_WEBHOOK_SECRET ) if (!isValidSignature) { return new NextResponse('Invalid signature', { status: 401 }) } revalidateTag('sanity') return NextResponse.json({ revalidated: true, now: Date.now() }) } catch (err: any) { return new NextResponse(err.message, { status: 500 }) } } ``` - In the Sanity dashboard → API → Webhooks, add a webhook pointing to `https://your-domain.com/api/revalidate` with the secret from `SANITY_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. - Tag all `client.fetch()` calls with `{ next: { tags: ['sanity'] } }` so revalidation clears the right cache: ```typescript const posts = await client.fetch(postsQuery, {}, { next: { tags: ['sanity'] } }) ``` ### 10. Render Portable Text (rich body content) - Install the renderer: ``` npm install @portabletext/react ``` - Use it in components: ```tsx import { PortableText } from '@portabletext/react' export function PostBody({ body }: { body: any[] }) { return } ``` - Customize renderers for custom block types (images, callouts, code blocks) by passing a `components` prop. ### 11. Verify the setup - Run `npm run dev` and open `http://localhost:3000/studio` — the Sanity Studio should load. - Create a test document in Studio, then open the page that fetches it. Content should appear. - Confirm the GROQ query returns the right shape using the Vision plugin inside Studio (under the toolbar). ### 12. Git hygiene - Add to `.gitignore`: ``` .env.local .sanity/ ``` - Commit schema files and `sanity.config.ts` — these are source code, not secrets. - NEVER commit `.env.local` — it contains `SANITY_API_READ_TOKEN`. ### Common errors & fixes - **Studio loads blank / 403 error** → CORS not configured. Go to Sanity dashboard → API → CORS Origins and add `http://localhost:3000` (and your production domain). - **`Cannot find module 'next-sanity/studio'`** → Ensure `next-sanity` version is ≥ 7. Run `npm install next-sanity@latest`. - **GROQ query returns `null`** → The document is in "draft" state in Sanity. Either publish it, or use the API token with drafts access (`useCdn: false` + `perspective: 'previewDrafts'`). - **Images return `null` from `@sanity/image-url`** → Pass the full image object from the query, not just the URL. Query must include the `asset` reference: `image { ..., asset->{ url } }`. - **Webhook revalidation returns 401** → `SANITY_WEBHOOK_SECRET` in Vercel/production does not match what was entered in the Sanity webhook config. Re-copy the secret. - **`_type` filter returns no results** → Check spelling — GROQ filters are case-sensitive. The schema name (`name: 'post'`) must match the query filter (`_type == "post"`). - **Studio route 404 in production** → The `[[...tool]]` catch-all must be inside `app/studio/`. Verify the directory structure matches exactly. ### What NOT to do - Do NOT import `@sanity/client` directly in Client Components — Sanity API tokens must stay server-side only. - Do NOT use `fetch` without `{ next: { tags: ['sanity'] } }` — you will miss cache invalidation from webhooks. - Do NOT skip CORS configuration — the Studio will silently fail to load documents without it. - Do NOT use `useCdn: true` when fetching draft content — the CDN only serves published documents. - Do NOT define schemas outside `schemaTypes/` — co-locating schemas keeps them discoverable and avoids import tangles. - Do NOT use `revalidatePath('/')` as a catch-all — tag-based revalidation (`revalidateTag`) is more precise and avoids unnecessary full-page cache busts.
